title: Nuclear Inspection Dispute Stalls US-Iran Peace Efforts
subtitle: Disagreements over when international experts can visit Iranian sites threaten a fragile regional ceasefire.

summary.en: The head of the United Nations nuclear agency insists that inspectors will soon visit Iranian nuclear sites. However, Iranian officials argue that such visits can only happen after a final peace deal is signed. This public disagreement highlights the fragile nature of the recent agreement between the two nations. Meanwhile, ongoing violence in the region continues to complicate diplomatic progress. Both sides now have a 60-day window to resolve these issues through private negotiations.
summary.zh: 聯合國核子機構負責人堅持視察人員很快將前往伊朗核設施進行視察。然而，伊朗官員主張，這類訪問必須在簽署最終和平協議後才能進行。這場公開的歧見凸顯了兩國近期協議的脆弱本質。與此同時，該地區持續的暴力事件不斷增加外交進展的複雜性。雙方目前有六十天的窗口期，透過私下談判來解決這些問題。

paragraphs.en: A new disagreement has emerged between the United Nations and Iran regarding the inspection of nuclear sites. Rafael Mariano Grossi, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) — the global organization responsible for monitoring nuclear activities — stated on Wednesday that his inspectors will visit Iran soon. This visit is part of an interim agreement recently signed by the United States and Iran to help end the war between the two nations.
paragraphs.zh: 聯合國與伊朗之間針對核設施視察一事出現了新的歧見。國際原子能總署（IAEA）署長葛羅西（Rafael Mariano Grossi）——該組織為負責監控全球核活動的機構——於週三表示，他的視察人員很快就會前往伊朗。此次訪問是美國與伊朗近期簽署的一項臨時協議的一部分，旨在協助終止兩國之間的戰爭。

paragraphs.en: However, officials in Tehran have a different view. According to NPR News, Iranian deputy foreign minister Kazem Gharibabadi insisted that any visit by international inspectors will only occur after a final, complete deal is reached. He stated that these issues will be decided only within the framework of a final agreement, which must also include the removal of sanctions — official penalties or restrictions placed on a country to force it to change its behavior.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，德黑蘭的官員持有不同看法。根據美國國家公共廣播電台（NPR）報導，伊朗外交部副部長加里巴阿巴迪（Kazem Gharibabadi）堅持認為，國際視察人員的任何訪問，都必須在達成最終且完整的協議後才會進行。他表示，這些議題只能在最終協議的框架內決定，而該協議也必須包含解除制裁——即為了迫使一國改變行為而對其施加的官方懲罰或限制措施。

paragraphs.en: This public conflict is part of a larger "war of words" between the two countries. Since the signing of the memorandum of understanding — a formal document outlining an agreement — leaders from both the U.S. and Iran have publicly disagreed about what the deal actually requires. Rafael Mariano Grossi noted that while he understands political statements are part of reality, the signed document clearly states that nuclear activities will be supervised by the IAEA. He emphasized that these inspections are going to happen, even if the exact timing is still being discussed.
paragraphs.zh: 這場公開衝突是兩國之間更大規模「口水戰」的一部分。自從簽署諒解備忘錄——即概述協議內容的正式文件——以來，美國與伊朗的領導人對於該協議的實際要求公開表達了不同意見。葛羅西指出，雖然他理解政治聲明是現實的一部分，但簽署的文件明確規定核活動將由國際原子能總署監督。他強調，即便確切時間仍在討論中，這些視察勢必會進行。

paragraphs.en: The tension over nuclear inspections is occurring at a difficult time. The region is currently trying to maintain a shaky ceasefire — an agreement to stop fighting for a period of time. This peace is threatened by ongoing violence, including a recent airstrike in southern Lebanon. NPR News reported that this strike killed two people, marking the first such attack since the latest ceasefire began on Saturday. While the U.S. and Iran have agreed to a 60-day period to work out the details of their deal, these violent incidents and public arguments make the path to peace very uncertain.
paragraphs.zh: 核視察引發的緊張局勢正值艱難時刻。該地區目前正試圖維持脆弱的停火協議——即一段時間內停止戰鬥的協議。這份和平正受到持續暴力的威脅，包括近期在黎巴嫩南部發生的空襲。美國國家公共廣播電台報導稱，這次空襲造成兩人死亡，這是自週六最新停火協議生效以來的首次此類攻擊。儘管美伊雙方已同意給予六十天的時間來敲定協議細節，但這些暴力事件與公開爭論使得通往和平的道路充滿不確定性。

paragraphs.en: At the heart of the issue is Iran’s nuclear program. Since a 12-day war began in 2025, the IAEA has been blocked from visiting Iranian enrichment sites. These are facilities where uranium is processed. Iran is the only country in the world to have uranium enriched to 60% purity without having a weapons program. While Iran maintains that its work is peaceful, the international community is concerned because the country is believed to store enough material to potentially build as many as 10 nuclear weapons. The current deal aims to have this uranium "downblended," or reduced in strength, to lower these risks.
paragraphs.zh: 問題的核心在於伊朗的核計畫。自二〇二五年十二天戰爭爆發以來，國際原子能總署一直被阻擋而無法進入伊朗的濃縮設施。這些設施是用來處理鈾礦的地方。伊朗是世界上唯一一個在沒有武器計畫的情況下，將鈾濃縮至百分之六十純度的國家。雖然伊朗堅稱其工作是和平的，但國際社會感到擔憂，因為據信該國儲存的材料足以製造多達十枚核武器。目前的協議旨在將這些鈾「降級」，也就是降低其濃度，以減少相關風險。

paragraphs.en: These diplomatic challenges are happening on several fronts. Beyond the nuclear issue, there is significant debate about how Iran will spend billions of dollars that will be unfrozen as part of the deal. The situation is further complicated by the ongoing conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, a militant group in Lebanon that receives support from Iran. Many observers worry that these overlapping crises could destroy the progress made in Switzerland, where the two sides have been meeting to negotiate.
paragraphs.zh: 這些外交挑戰在多個層面上同時發生。除了核議題外，關於伊朗將如何運用協議中解凍的數十億美元資金，也存在重大爭議。以色列與黎巴嫩激進組織真主黨（Hezbollah）之間持續的衝突，使局勢變得更加複雜，而該組織正接受伊朗的支援。許多觀察家擔心，這些重疊的危機可能會摧毀雙方在瑞士進行談判所取得的進展。

paragraphs.en: Despite the public disagreements, the 60-day window remains a critical time for both nations. The U.S. and Iran are expected to use this time to hold private talks to resolve their differences. However, the tone of the negotiations remains tense. Iranian officials have criticized the U.S. approach, suggesting that the current policies are not helping to build trust. As the two sides continue to negotiate in public, the risk of the entire peace process falling apart remains high.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管存在公開分歧，這六十天的窗口期對兩國而言仍是關鍵時刻。預計美伊雙方將利用這段時間進行私下會談，以解決彼此的歧見。然而，談判的基調依然緊張。伊朗官員批評美國的做法，暗示目前的政策無助於建立信任。隨著雙方持續在公開場合進行談判，整個和平進程破裂的風險依然很高。

paragraphs.en: For now, the world is watching to see if the IAEA will be granted access to the sites. Rafael Mariano Grossi remains firm, stating that the exact date of the visit is less important than the fact that it will happen. Whether this happens in a few days or a few weeks, the success of the broader peace deal may depend on the transparency of Iran’s nuclear program. Until then, the international community remains cautious as they wait to see if the two sides can move past their differences and secure a lasting peace.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，全世界都在關注國際原子能總署是否能獲准進入這些設施。葛羅西立場堅定，表示訪問的確切日期不如訪問本身會發生來得重要。無論這是在幾天還是幾週後發生，更廣泛和平協議的成功可能取決於伊朗核計畫的透明度。在此之前，國際社會仍保持謹慎，等待觀察雙方是否能跨越分歧並確保長久的和平。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation where two sides argue publicly, often through statements or media, rather than through physical fighting.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個慣用語，字面上是「言語的戰爭」，指雙方透過公開言論互相指責。文中：This public conflict is part of a larger "war of words" between the two countries.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Different areas of activity or different problems that are being dealt with at the same time.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（前線/正面），這裡指同時處理的多個議題或挑戰。文中：These diplomatic challenges are happening on several fronts.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To fail, break down, or come to an end, especially regarding a plan, agreement, or relationship.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作實體物體的「散架」，這裡指抽象的和平進程失敗。文中：As the two sides continue to negotiate in public, the risk of the entire peace process falling apart remains high.

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: U.N. nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran, but Iran says only after final deal

previousArticles.title: Nuclear Inspections Remain a Point of Conflict in US-Iran Peace Talks
previousArticles.summaryEn: The head of the UN nuclear agency confirms that inspectors will visit Iranian sites as part of a recent peace deal. However, Iranian officials continue to argue that such access will only be granted after a final agreement is reached. The disagreement highlights the ongoing tension between the two nations following their recent conflict. Meanwhile, US officials are traveling through the Gulf to reassure regional allies about the security of the deal. The situation remains complex as both sides continue to negotiate in public while trying to maintain a fragile ceasefire.

previousArticles.title: US and Iran Seek Path to Peace Amid New Tensions
previousArticles.summaryEn: High-stakes talks between the United States and Iran continue in Switzerland despite significant challenges. President Donald Trump’s aggressive social media posts caused a temporary walkout by the Iranian delegation. Meanwhile, the US has issued a temporary waiver to ease oil sanctions on Iran. Both sides have agreed to a 60-day roadmap to reach a final deal. However, disagreements remain regarding nuclear inspections and regional military actions.

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the specific condition Iranian official Kazem Gharibabadi set for allowing IAEA inspectors into the country?

options.A: The inspectors must wait for the 60-day negotiation period to expire.
options.B: A final, comprehensive agreement must be reached first.
options.C: The United States must first provide proof that Iran is building weapons.
options.D: The IAEA must promise to remove all existing sanctions immediately.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，伊朗副外長 Kazem Gharibabadi 明確表示，國際檢查員的訪問必須在達成最終且完整的協議後才會發生。選項 A 錯誤，因為 60 天是談判期限，並非檢查的先決條件。選項 C 錯誤，文章並未提到美國需提供伊朗製造武器的證據。選項 D 錯誤，伊朗要求的是協議必須包含解除制裁，而非要求檢查機構（IAEA）本身去解除制裁。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current relationship between the U.S. and Iran based on the text?

options.A: They have successfully built trust through recent private negotiations.
options.B: They are in total agreement regarding the timeline of nuclear inspections.
options.C: They are struggling to reconcile their public rhetoric with the formal documents they signed.
options.D: They have decided to stop all public communication to avoid further conflict.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第三段，雖然雙方簽署了諒解備忘錄，但兩國領導人對協議內容的解讀公開不一致，且 IAEA 主席 Grossi 指出雙方對核查的觀點存在分歧，顯示雙方在「公開言論」與「簽署文件」之間存在落差。選項 A 錯誤，文章提到雙方缺乏信任。選項 B 錯誤，雙方對檢查時間點有明顯爭議。選項 D 錯誤，文章明確提到雙方仍在進行公開爭論。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'downblended' mean in the context of Iran's uranium?

options.A: To be mixed with other chemicals to make it invisible.
options.B: To be reduced in concentration or potency.
options.C: To be moved to a different storage facility.
options.D: To be sold to international markets for profit.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第五段上下文，伊朗將鈾濃縮至 60% 純度，而目前的協議目標是將其「downblended」，目的是降低風險，這意味著要降低其濃縮程度或效力。選項 A、C、D 皆與降低核材料威脅的語境不符。該詞在此處意指將高純度物質稀釋，使其危險性降低。

quiz.question: What is the primary theme of this article?

options.A: The technical process of how uranium is enriched to 60% purity.
options.B: The history of the 12-day war that began in 2025.
options.C: The ongoing challenges and uncertainty surrounding the nuclear peace deal between the U.S. and Iran.
options.D: The role of the IAEA in monitoring global nuclear weapons programs.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文全文圍繞著美國與伊朗之間脆弱的和平進程，探討了核查分歧、暴力事件、外交角力及互信不足等問題，核心主旨即為兩國在達成核協議過程中所面臨的重重困難與不確定性。選項 A 僅是背景細節，選項 B 提及的戰爭僅是背景，選項 D 範圍過大，未聚焦於美伊目前的特定衝突。
